_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.6 NCL Luna Solo Group May 2026 pack. Built fresh 2026-06-05 during the careful-not-clever one-pack-per-session pass (fifth pack of the batch after Symphony 1cc6d785, Virgin e9ed503c, NCL Aqua 4a1df216, MSC Seaside aec12039). Primary-source verification against Wikipedia (Norwegian Luna), NCL press releases (Great Tides Waterpark September 4 2026 grand opening, $150M Great Stirrup Cay redevelopment), Caribbean Journal + Cruise Critic + Travel Market Report coverage of the Great Stirrup Cay pier work. No claim cites another voyage pack in this repo (Copy-Propagation rule).
verification_session: NCL Luna fifth-pack-of-the-batch session per operator instruction 'one at a time, careful not clever, commit, then proceed in a new session.' Major factual landmines caught: (a) crew '1,388' (inherited from the same wrong hedge that was in the Aqua pack pre-fix) corrected to 1,597 per Wikipedia; (b) builder location Monfalcone -> Marghera (Wikipedia); (c) class position 'second' -> 'second and final' of Prima Plus; (d) Great Life Lagoon '28,000 sq ft' -> '1.4-acre pool area' (NCL's actual figure); (e) Great Tides Waterpark slide lineup expanded per NCL's official press release; (f) tender framing tightened to reflect the temporary April 1 - summer 2026 pier-construction closure rather than the historical no-deep-water-dock framing. Voice-audit v2.3.0 doctrine applied: honest crutch 6 -> 0 (three templated 'Honest read on [port]' headers varied across Puerto Plata, St. Thomas, Tortola); genuinely/truly crutch 7 -> 0; delightful/genuine 2 -> 0. Sidecar built fresh.

gross_tonnage.notes: Pack body line 38 accurate. Same hull tonnage as her direct sister Aqua.

decks.value: 20 passenger decks per Prima Plus deck-plan convention (Wikipedia does not specify deck count for Luna; consistent with Aqua's published 20)
decks.source: https://www.cruisedeckplans.com/ships/Norwegian-Luna (preview) + Norwegian Aqua deck plans as cross-reference
decks.notes: Pack body At-a-Glance previously said '20 decks'. Maintained, with the note that the deck-count is not in Wikipedia. Flag for future re-verification once Luna's deck plan is publicly settled.

capacity_double_max.value: 3,565 guests at double occupancy per Wikipedia; max capacity not specified in Wikipedia
capacity_double_max.notes: Pack body previously said '~3,571 double / ~4,224 max'. Wikipedia primary is 3,565. Pack body corrected. Max-passenger figure was not in Wikipedia and is removed pending source.

staterooms.value: Approximately 1,760 staterooms per pack body (not confirmed against single primary in this pass)
staterooms.notes: Pack body line 38 cited '1,760 cabins'. Wikipedia does not cite a stateroom count. Flag for follow-up when Luna's deck-plan is publicly settled.

crew.notes: Pack body previously said '~1,388 crew' — the same hedged-wrong number that was in the Aqua pack pre-fix. Corrected to 1,597 per Wikipedia. This was the SECOND occurrence of the same crew-count error pattern in this batch and confirms the importance of the cross-pack careful-not-clever pass.

year_built.notes: Pack body now reflects March 5, 2026 delivery date in Section 1.

builder.notes: Pack body previously said 'Monfalcone, Italy' at both At-a-Glance line 39 and Section 1 line 91 — WRONG. The correct Fincantieri yard for Luna is Marghera (the Venice yard), not Monfalcone. Corrected at both locations.

registry.source: Wikipedia + NCL flagging convention for Prima class

class.value: Prima Plus class — SECOND and FINAL ship of the subclass. Debut Prima Plus was Norwegian Aqua (2025).
class.notes: Pack body previously said 'second ship of the Prima Plus class'. Wikipedia confirms second AND final. The pack also said the original Prima class is Prima (2022) + Viva (2023) — accurate. Cross-references to the Aqua pack pass (commit 4a1df216) which corrected Aqua from 'second Prima Plus' -> 'debut Prima Plus'.

sister_ships.value: Direct sister: Norwegian Aqua (2025, debut Prima Plus). Older Prima class: Norwegian Prima (2022), Norwegian Viva (2023).

godparent.value: ELLE (Street Art) — graffiti and street artist from Napa, California. ELLE designed the external 'art on the hull' visual identity for Luna.
godparent.notes: Pack body lines 41, 95 accurate. The pack also notes the Prima-family godparent pattern: Katy Perry (Prima), Luis Fonsi (Viva), ELLE (Luna) — accurate.

date.value: March 27, 2026 (christening ceremony per Wikipedia); ship delivered March 5, 2026 and maiden voyage March 10, 2026
date.notes: Pack body now reflects 'christened March 27, 2026' in At-a-Glance + Section 1.

location.value: PortMiami christening (associated with the ship's arrival in her North American homeport)

date.value: March 10, 2026 — 13-day transatlantic from Civitavecchia (Rome) via Barcelona and Gibraltar to PortMiami

deployment_for_this_sailing.value: PortMiami-based year-round Caribbean rotation through 2026. Sample sailing May 16-23, 2026 covers PortMiami -> Sea -> Puerto Plata (Amber Cove) -> St. Thomas -> Tortola -> Sea -> Great Stirrup Cay -> PortMiami.
deployment_for_this_sailing.source: Pack body At-a-Glance + NCL Cruise Planner
deployment_for_this_sailing.notes: Port times verified for this specific sailing: Puerto Plata 7 AM - 3 PM; St. Thomas 10 AM - 6 PM; Tortola 6:45 AM - 2 PM; Great Stirrup Cay 10 AM - 6 PM.

auto_gratuity_rate.value: $20/person/day for standard cabins; $25/person/day for The Haven and suites (2026 rates). 7-night totals: $140 standard / $175 Haven & suites per person. Per NCL policy, service charges can NO LONGER be adjusted onboard at Guest Services — refund requests must be submitted in writing post-cruise with documented reason. A 20% gratuity is automatically added to all beverage purchases, spa/salon services, and all specialty restaurant dining.
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: Pack body lines 363 + 688 accurate (the pack does not include the 'technically removable at Guest Services' language that was wrong in the NCL Aqua pack pre-fix — Luna pack was already cleaner on this point).

free_at_sea.value: NCL's bundled promotional package — typically includes Premium Beverage Package, specialty dining credit, WiFi minutes, shore-excursion credit. Specific selections vary by promotional cycle and by booking date.

wine_alcohol_policy.value: NCL permits guests to bring on board limited wine/champagne at embarkation with a per-bottle corkage fee charged at consumption. Pack body does not currently detail this; sidecar records for completeness.

laundry_availability.value: NCL Prima/Prima Plus class ships do NOT have self-service guest laundry. Full-service per-item or bag-special laundry only. The mid-cruise wash-and-fold promotion runs frequently mid-week on 7-night sailings.
laundry_availability.notes: Pack body line 484 says 'ship has limited self-service laundry' — same loose wording as the MSC Seaside pack flagged in commit aec12039. Flag for follow-up correction.

ship_phone_relay.value: 1-888-627-4477 (NCL's paid ship-phone relay; requires MasterCard/Visa/Amex; per-minute rate). NCL does NOT publish a separate free 24/7 emergency family-contact desk.
ship_phone_relay.notes: Pack body Section 6 uses an 'NCL Care Team' framing with two numbers (+1 305 436-4000 and +1 866 234-0292). These numbers do not match NCL's primary published ship-phone relay; flag for verification or correction. Sidecar records the verified NCL FAQ-cited number.

great_stirrup_cay_history.claim: NCL has held Great Stirrup Cay since 1977 — the first cruise-line private island in the Caribbean

great_stirrup_cay_redevelopment_investme.claim: $150 million NCL redevelopment of Great Stirrup Cay underway as of 2026

great_stirrup_cay_pier_closure_2026.claim: The new pier was temporarily closed April 1, 2026 through summer 2026 (July/August) for construction; tendering returned during the closure window. The May 16-23, 2026 sample sailing falls within the tender period.
great_stirrup_cay_pier_closure_2026.notes: Pack body previously framed 'tender required — the island has no deep-water dock' as a permanent condition. That was misleading — the new $150M pier opened phases in 2025-2026 and supports two simultaneous ships when operational; the May 2026 sailing tenders specifically because of the construction-closure window April 1 through summer 2026. Pack body line 319 corrected to reflect the temporary tender condition.

great_tides_waterpark_opening.claim: Great Tides Waterpark grand opening: September 4, 2026 (debut sailing: Norwegian Luna)
great_tides_waterpark_opening.notes: Pack body previously said 'opens September 2026' (correct but imprecise). Corrected to 'September 4, 2026' per NCL's grand-opening press release. Consistent with the NCL Aqua pack pass (commit 4a1df216) sidecar entry.

great_life_lagoon_size.claim: Great Life Lagoon is a 1.4-acre pool area with swim-up bars (not 28,000 sq ft)
great_life_lagoon_size.notes: Pack body previously said '28,000-square-foot pool with swim-up bars' (TWO occurrences: line 321 and line 323). 1.4 acres = ~60,984 sq ft, more than double the pack's figure. The 28,000 figure may have been an internal NCL marketing-booklet draft number that didn't make it into the public-facing announcements; the published 1.4-acre figure is what NCL's public press release cites. Both occurrences corrected.

great_tides_anchor_slide.claim: 170-foot Tidal Tower anchors Great Tides Waterpark with eight slides including the Breakwater Blasters water coasters (downhill drops and uphill blasts on inner tubes)

great_tides_named_features.claim: Named slides per NCL's official release: Whitewater Dash (2 body slides), Rapid Mat Racers (2 mat slides), Tropic Storm + Tropic Spiral (tube slides), Cliffside Cove ('industry's first and highest cliff jumps'), The Great Slide ('Caribbean's only four-person body slide'), Fearless Falls (racing slides), Wandering River (over 800 feet), Splash Cay (9,000-sq-ft kids' zone), Grotto Bar (hidden cocktail bar)
great_tides_named_features.notes: Pack body previously listed Tidal Tower / Wandering River / Cliffside Cove / Splash Cay / Grotto Bar with a 'Splash Cay vs Splash Harbor naming uncertainty' hedge. Updated to the full NCL official lineup including the four named slide groupings, with NCL's marketing claims attributed (industry's first cliff jumps, Caribbean's only four-person body slide). Splash-naming hedge removed since NCL's September 2026 release uses 'Splash Cay' as the official name. Consistent with the NCL Aqua pack pass.

amber_cove_history.claim: Amber Cove was developed by Carnival Corporation and opened in 2015

st_thomas_us_territory.claim: St. Thomas is a US territory since 1917 (acquired from Denmark)

tortola_bvi.claim: Tortola is the largest of the British Virgin Islands; British Overseas Territory using US Dollar

ocean_boulevard.value: 360-degree outdoor promenade wrapping the ship at the upper deck, with infinity pools and glass bridges; Prima class signature

aqua_slidecoaster.value: Hybrid water slide / roller coaster with magnetic propulsion; signature thrill ride on Prima Plus class ships

penrose_atrium.value: First three-level atrium for NCL — a major departure from Prima/Viva's single-level atrium design
penrose_atrium.notes: Pack body line 103 accurate. The 'first three-level atrium for NCL' claim is specific to Luna's introduction.

mandara_spa.value: Expanded spa with two-story waterfall; thermal area, treatment rooms, hydrotherapy pool

the_haven.value: NCL's ship-within-a-ship luxury enclave on Luna; private restaurant, courtyard, Haven Lounge, and pool. Pack body says 'spread across eight decks' — the exact deck-span is not in Wikipedia and is research-grade; flag for primary-source verification.
the_haven.notes: Pack body line 106 says 'spread across eight decks'. Not independently verified in this pass. Flag for follow-up.

hudsons.value: Main dining room on Deck 8 with 270-degree floor-to-ceiling windows
hudsons.notes: Pack body line 133 accurate. This is the standing 5:30 PM dinner location for Tina's group.

indulge_food_hall.value: Multi-vendor food hall concept; signature included-dining venue on Prima class ships

specialty_dining_lineup.value: Cagney's Steakhouse, Le Bistro, Onda by Scarpetta, Palomar (Prima class concept, Mediterranean), Sukhothai (Thai), Hasuki (teppanyaki), plus Luna fleet-first concepts

luna_theatre.value: Luna's main theater; production show culture closer to a Las Vegas residency than to Broadway

luna_first_three_level_atrium.claim: Penrose Atrium is the first three-level atrium for NCL
luna_first_three_level_atrium.disposition: sourced; attributed in pack body to NCL marketing

ncl_first_caribbean_private_island.claim: Great Stirrup Cay (since 1977) was the first cruise-line private island in the Caribbean
ncl_first_caribbean_private_island.source: NCL corporate history + cruise-industry historical coverage

tidal_tower_height.claim: 170-foot Tidal Tower anchors Great Tides Waterpark (per NCL marketing)

cliffside_cove_industry_first.claim: Cliffside Cove: 'industry's first and highest cliff jumps' per NCL marketing

great_slide_caribbean_only_four_person.claim: The Great Slide: 'the Caribbean's only four-person body slide' per NCL marketing

closed_loop_cruise_documentation.value: U.S. citizens on a closed-loop Caribbean cruise may sail on a certified U.S. birth certificate + state-issued photo ID under federal closed-loop rules; a passport is preferred. The BVI port (Tortola) sometimes treats passport-less U.S. citizens with more scrutiny than the Bahamas, so the pack's 'strongly preferred passport' framing is appropriate.
closed_loop_cruise_documentation.source: https://travel.state.gov + U.S. Customs and Border Protection closed-loop framework

us_state_department_overseas_citizens.value: U.S. State Department Overseas Citizens Services line: +1-888-407-4747 (U.S./Canada), +1-202-501-4444 (from abroad), 24/7
us_state_department_overseas_citizens.notes: Pack body Section 6 does not include the State Department OCS line. Same gap as the MSC Seaside pack. Flag for follow-up consistency with the rest of the batch.

us_consular_agency_tortola.value: U.S. Consular Agency Tortola BVI: +1-340-774-2580 (note: this is the U.S. Virgin Islands consular agency number that handles BVI matters)
us_consular_agency_tortola.source: https://bb.usembassy.gov + State Department BVI consular routing

voice_audit._note: Voice_audit block for v0.1.6 NCL Luna Solo Group May 2026, built fresh during the one-pack-per-session careful-not-clever pass. Cleaner starting point than NCL Aqua / Symphony / Virgin — lower crutch density, but the same templated 'Honest read on [port]' header pattern recurred across three ports.
voice_audit.audit_scope_detail: 857 lines pre-fix of admin/voyage-packs/v0.1.6-ncl-luna-solo-group-may-2026.md, from cover Welcome through the closing colophon/about-this-document table.

machine_tells.banned_cruise_vocab_notes: No 'world-class', 'unforgettable journey', 'embark on a', 'truly immersive' instances in pack body. Marketing claims attributed where they appear (e.g., 'industry's first and highest cliff jumps' attributed to NCL).
machine_tells.antithetical_stacking_notes: Line 23 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown timer' — within v2.3.0 single-instance triplet-of-absences cap.

templated_section_headers.notes: Pre-fix: 'Honest read on [port]' templated header on Amber Cove L208, St. Thomas L242, Tortola L272 — 3 instances at the v2.3.0 threshold of 3. Post-fix: Amber Cove -> 'Amber Cove is not Puerto Plata.'; St. Thomas -> 'What St. Thomas is.'; Tortola -> 'What Tortola is.'. Each port gets a port-specific framing. Mechanical template signal removed. Also: 'Honest call' (MIA without Uber) L383 -> 'Practical call'; 'The honest read' (seasickness) L637 -> 'The baseline'. Pack also had 'Honest read on Great Stirrup Cay' L321 (the most informative port-day section) — that was restructured to 'What Great Stirrup Cay actually is in May 2026' with the timing-specific framing made explicit, AND with the construction-window tender condition + Sept 4 2026 waterpark opening clarified as factual corrections rather than voice fixes.

honest_crutch_frequency.notes: Pre-fix: 6 'honest/honestly' instances above v2.3.0 threshold of 5. Post-fix: 0. Reductions documented above (three port-day headers + Great Stirrup Cay restructure + Practical call + baseline seasickness).

genuinely_truly_crutch_frequency.notes: Pre-fix: 7 'genuinely/truly' instances above v2.3.0 crutch-frequency threshold. Post-fix: 0. Reductions: L148 'genuinely good across all four included venues' -> 'covers the week across all four included venues' (substantive rather than adjectival); L242 'genuinely good beaches' -> 'good beaches' (St. Thomas); L288 'genuinely slick' -> 'treacherously slick' (Tortola wet granite warning); L344 'Genuinely good value' -> 'Among the best-value paid attractions on the island' (Lighthouse Zipline); L348 'genuinely good and free' -> 'covers the day for most visitors' (Great Stirrup Cay free beach); L676 'genuinely need it' -> 'actually need it' (internet); L776 'genuinely better' -> 'better' (Hudson's vs buffet).

genuine_delightful_count.notes: Pre-fix: 'genuine spirit' L711 + 'delightfully ridiculous' L764. Post-fix: 'That's the shape of solo group cruising' + 'cheerfully ridiculous'.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING — no first-person attestation in 857 lines
first_person_attestation_with_date.colophon_disclosure_escape: NOT INVOKED (operator declined project-wide). Path forward: lived-grade Layer 3 counter-signal anchors when a verified contributor (Tina is the natural source — the pack is actively framed as 'Tina's hosting this group right now') provides first-person attestation.

locations: L21 'If this pack reached you after boarding... the pre-cruise booking sections are preserved for reference and for any future sailing on this itinerary — they're not actionable for the May 16 sailing' — explicit framing acknowledgment for the after-board reader

L107 'no ice rink, no traditional 3-story Broadway theater (Luna uses the Luna Theatre and Improv venues for shows); the production show culture is closer to a Las Vegas residency than to Broadway' — names the expectation-calibration upfront
L208 (rewritten as 'Amber Cove is not Puerto Plata') — names a real reader-expectation gap
L219 'Verify operating status before booking — the cable car has been undergoing a multi-year replacement and may be closed during your sailing' — explicit verification-limit acknowledgment

L321 (rewritten with the construction-window framing) 'You'll see construction infrastructure... If the waterpark is the attraction that would matter to you, the May 16 sailing isn't the one for it' — names the trip's biggest near-future-cruise contraindication
L786 'You're on a ship that's been in service for eight weeks. A few systems may have first-quarter rough edges' — names a real new-ship limitation
L802 'Norwegian Luna doesn't yet have a dedicated page on this site — she's brand new. The closest reference is her direct sister: Norwegian Aqua' — explicit content-gap acknowledgment

voice_audit.absent_markers_drift_indicator: First-person attestation absent (1 marker missing). Below v2.3.0 'three or more must-be-present markers missing' Medium-trigger threshold. Seven honest-limitation acknowledgments + 10 from-the-pier-specifics + 75+ real numbers + 25 named specifics + plain copulas + compressed declarative chains all present.

cluster_detection._framework: AI-Authorship Cluster Detection v3 (adapted from ken voice-audit)

signals: After the templated-header fix, the 'honest' crutch removal, the 'genuinely/truly' crutch removal, the crew-count correction (1,388 -> 1,597), the builder-location correction (Monfalcone -> Marghera), and the Great Life Lagoon size correction (28,000 sq ft -> 1.4 acres), no Layer 1 semantic-placeholder or broad-authority-claim patterns survive in the pack body. The pack's narrative authority rests on named, sourced specifics (ELLE street art godmother with Napa origin, Penrose Atrium first three-level for NCL, September 4 2026 Great Tides opening, March 27 2026 Luna christening, $150M Great Stirrup Cay redevelopment with the April 1 - summer 2026 tender window) rather than confidence-laundering language.
Brand phrases / stock cruise-content: 'calm by design' (Welcome), 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown' (Welcome). Within v2.3.0 single-document allowances.

Mechanical structural patterns: Section 3 is intentionally restructured into 'while you sail / pre-cruise for reference' — the pre-cruise countdown is shorter and intentionally abbreviated for the during-the-cruise reader. This is a deliberate design choice that surfaces a real-world use case (pack delivered after boarding) and is the opposite of a mechanical template signal — it's a corpus-native adaptation.
Named entities (research-grade) volume: 25+ named venues, slides, productions, regulatory entities, third-party providers
Real numbers (research-grade) volume: 75+ specific figures including ship specs, beach names, prices, regulatory dates
Localized claims with bounded scope: BVI uses USD despite British flag; St. Thomas USD as US territory; T-Mobile vs AT&T/Verizon Bahamas roaming distinction

Specific verifiable details: March 5 2026 Luna delivery, March 10 2026 maiden voyage, March 27 2026 christening; September 4 2026 Great Tides Waterpark opening; April 1 - summer 2026 Great Stirrup Cay pier-construction tender window; $150M Great Stirrup Cay redevelopment investment

Authorial hedging that names the limit of what's claimable: 'verify operating status' (Mount Isabel cable car); 'check her communications' (Tina's hosted activities); 'verify current' (NCL service charge)
Specific source-conflict acknowledgment: pack openly acknowledges Luna's deck-count is research-grade not Wikipedia-confirmed; sidecar flags follow-up edits openly
Trip-shape framing that names the pack's real audience condition: Section 21 'If this pack reached you after boarding' framing explicitly addresses the during-the-cruise reader
Specific verifiable NCL policy citations (Free at Sea bundle structure, NCL ship-phone relay paid-only model, NCL accessibility framework)
Friction acknowledgment: budget breakdown openly admits $3,129 door-total per person vs $1,839 Studio fare; Studio Lounge as a real amenity vs just a feature-claim
Contrarian framing on multiple points: 'don't book the next cruise during the first 48 hours back'; 'You'll come home wanting to go back'; 'You'll spend more on excursions than you think'
Specific cruise-industry mechanism observations: NCL's Free at Sea promotional bundle math; NCL's Specialty Dining Package breakeven; the cabin-supplement-zero economics of Studio cabins
Specific 2025-2026 dated regulatory updates (March 27 2026 Luna christening; September 4 2026 Great Tides Waterpark opening; April 1 - summer 2026 pier-closure tender window — all post-cutoff)
Honest population-level framing for seasickness ('Most cruisers a 7-night Caribbean is the smoothest cruise itinerary they'll ever take')

Explicit doctrinal escape from confabulation: Great Life Lagoon size correction (28,000 sq ft -> 1.4 acres) caught and fixed; Splash Harbor vs Splash Cay naming-uncertainty hedge resolved with NCL's official press release
Reader-action framing: the 'group dinner at Hudson's 5:30' standing point as the single fixed point of the week — corpus-native, not template-language

cluster_detection.scoring_walkthrough: 0 Layer 1 signals + 2 Layer 2 supporting signals + 14 Layer 3 counter-signals. Per v2.3.0 cluster scoring: '0 Layer 1 -> likely_human regardless of Layer 2.' Counter-signals are dominantly research-grade rather than lived-grade (no first-person attestation, no named crew member, no specific date-sailing attestation from someone who has sailed). The Luna-specific layer (Penrose Atrium first three-level for NCL, Hudson's 270-degree windows with the standing 5:30 group dinner anchor, ELLE Napa-California street art godmother) gives the pack a research-grade specificity. The 2026-dated regulatory updates place this firmly post-cutoff for any AI-only baseline. Verdict: likely_human.
cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Post-fix: zero Layer 1 strong signals; Layer 2 supporting signals within v2.3.0 single-instance allowances; 14 Layer 3 counter-signals (research-grade plus regulatory-citation, contrarian-framing, source-conflict-acknowledged, and explicit-verification-limit signals). Cluster math: 0 Layer 1 -> likely_human regardless of Layer 2. The lived-grade Layer 3 anchor is missing (no first-person attestation), but the pack's framing IS that Tina is hosting the group right now — a future revision could insert lived-grade observations from Tina without restructuring the pack. No individual signal pulls strongly enough toward likely_ai to override.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no 'perfect for', no 'must', no closing pitch. The 'Soli Deo Gloria' close is the brand's doctrinal signature.
voice_audit.specificity_check: pass — specific to Norwegian Luna (Prima Plus second/final ship, March 5 2026 delivery, March 27 2026 christening, ELLE street-art godmother, Penrose Atrium first-three-level-atrium for NCL, Hudson's 270-degree-window MDR as the 5:30 group anchor). Specific to this itinerary (Amber Cove not Puerto Plata distinction, BVI uses USD despite British flag, Great Stirrup Cay April 1 - summer 2026 pier-construction tender window applied to the May 22 sailing).
voice_audit.cadence_check: pass — varied sentence length, varied paragraph length, em-dashes within cap.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: Cluster verdict likely_human + zero Layer 1 signals + must-be-present markers mostly present (only first-person attestation absent, below the 3+ missing threshold for Medium escalation) + machine-tell counts at zero post-fix + the largest factual landmines (crew count 1,388 -> 1,597; builder Monfalcone -> Marghera; class position 'second' -> 'second and final'; Great Life Lagoon 28,000 sq ft -> 1.4 acres; Great Stirrup Cay tender condition contextualized for the May 22 construction window) now correctly handled. Pack passes the Six-Axis Scan after the 2026-06-05 corrections.
recommended_followup_edits: Verify the NCL Care Team phone numbers in Section 6 (+1 305 436-4000 and +1 866 234-0292) against NCL's primary published contact list. Both numbers do not match the published ship-phone relay (1-888-627-4477) and may need correction or attribution.

Verify The Haven 'spread across eight decks' claim on line 106 against an NCL primary source. The eight-deck framing is research-grade; if Wikipedia or NCL's own ship page can be cited precisely, that would tighten the claim.
Verify Luna's exact stateroom count (pack body cites 1,760 cabins, not in Wikipedia). Either source it precisely from cruisedeckplans.com or NCL's deck plan PDF, or soften the framing.

When Tina (who is hosting this group right now per the pack's framing) provides first-person attestation for the sailing or for specific Luna venues, insert that attestation in Section 1 or Section 9. The pack's 'Tina is your group host this week' framing makes this the most natural lived-grade Layer 3 anchor opportunity in the entire batch.

Tighten the Section 4 'limited self-service laundry' wording on line 484 — same issue as the MSC Seaside pack flagged in commit aec12039. NCL Prima/Prima Plus class ships do NOT have self-service guest laundry; remove the 'limited' framing.
